title: The Cavalry of Rome
designIntent: DSK-composed WebGPU-first fidelity route with no in-game UI DOM: scan over a larger pannable realistic Roman terrain map, hover oversized highlighted regions, click to trigger a cinematic dive, and reveal a primitive-built full-bodied battlefield tableau with a procedural vegetation population pass.
standingIterationRule: Every future gameplay iteration should improve visual fidelity as a secondary goal and should not regress terrain density, vegetation, lighting mood, region readability, soldier fidelity, or cinematic composition.

currentGameplayStep.intent: Move toward scene-native gameplay by removing HUD, footer, command UI, and hidden UI DOM surfaces from the in-game presentation.
currentGameplayStep.implementation: Live and experiment HTML now contain only the app root, canvas, loader, modules, and a non-DOM CavalryUiSinkShim for runtime status/readout compatibility. No hud/footer/commandBar DOM elements remain.

fidelityFocus.terrain: realistic low-poly terrain with domain warping, FBM/ridged noise, blended biome colors, moisture/slope/river masks, and non-repeating landforms
fidelityFocus.mapNavigation: large pannable region-selection surface with keyboard/drag pan and wheel zoom
fidelityFocus.regions: oversized highlighted region meshes with glow, wobble borders, and DSK affordance descriptors
fidelityFocus.uiDom: none; runtime status hooks are non-DOM sinks only

proceduralVegetation.grass: dense deterministic grass descriptor field with wind response
proceduralVegetation.trees: biome-aware primitive groves and tree clusters
proceduralVegetation.flowersAndRocks: small wildflower and rock breakup details
proceduralVegetation.boundary: local procedural vegetation descriptor DSK candidate owns placements; overlay renderer only presents descriptors

fidelityFocus.soldiers: full-bodied primitive soldiers built from boxes/quads/triangles: legs, boots, torso, cuirass, arms, head, helmet, crest, shield, spear, cape, shadow
fidelityFocus.battlefield: primitive formations, banners, vegetation foreground, field strokes, trampled center lane, atmospheric fog

protectedExpressionGuardrails: Do not copy tabletop scenario layouts, card systems, dice faces, command sections, rule text, or unit-stat tables.
Keep the route visual-first until the cinematic terrain and battlefield proof is stable.
Use Roman-history flavor as original visual composition, not as a digital recreation of a board-game ruleset.
currentLocalSurfaces: realistic terrain mesh color and brush-stroke overlay generation
large pannable region ellipse placement and labels
procedural vegetation descriptor generation and overlay presentation
cinematic camera interpolation and map pan/zoom controls
non-DOM runtime sink shim for status/readout compatibility

dskOwned: procedural vegetation descriptor snapshot via local DSK candidate

futureProtoKitCandidatesFromCustomLogic.reason: Reusable visual-material layer for painterly map surfaces.

futureProtoKitCandidatesFromCustomLogic.reason: Reusable replacement for point-node strategy maps.

futureProtoKitCandidatesFromCustomLogic.reason: Reusable renderer-neutral vegetation population layer for high-fidelity terrain routes.
Reusable mapping from terrain conditions to plant families and density budgets.

formation layout, banner placement, shadow placement, and battlefield tableau staging

nextLedge: Add scene-native gameplay interaction with no UI DOM: use terrain/region/army affordances directly, while preserving the standing visual-fidelity improvement rule.
